notes_for_model: Use modern Hebrew, secular register as default. Israeli directness is famously high — do not soften pro forma; bluntness reads as honesty, hedging reads as evasion. Distinguish secular (default), traditional (מסורתי), religious (דתי), ultra-Orthodox (חרדי) markers in the message corpus and adjust register accordingly: religious-context users may need slightly softer framing and respect for Shabbat / Kashrut / prayer-time references as evidence not topic. Output direction is RTL — frontend handles, but check that embedded Latin numbers and Latin brand marks do not break bidi flow. If subject gender is unclear, do NOT default — Hebrew is heavily gender-marked and Israeli readers are gender-aware; prefer to surface the ambiguity explicitly ('המשתמש/ת' or describe behaviour without forcing pronoun). IDF service is shared experience for most Israeli adults; references to military service appear as background frame, not topic. Holocaust references when present in user content are sober context, never casual. Russian-speaking Israeli users may switch RU/HE mid-sentence — accept and analyse both. Therapy vocabulary (PTSD, gaslighting, boundaries, attachment) is mainstream and acceptable. When discussing growth or repair themes, the תיקון framing resonates culturally. Brand mark 'tells' and 'vøiddo' stay Latin — never transliterate to «טלס».
